rulings.Case Title: JACQUELINE FRANCIS VS. LEN FREEMAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel From Defendants Tracy Dearman And Tcgsf, Inc. Discovery Responses To Special And Form Interrogatories, Requests For Document Production And To Produce Documents
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, April 8, 2026, Line 1, PLAINTIFF JACQUELINE FRANCIS' Motion To Compel From Defendants Tracy Dearman And Tcgsf, Inc. Discovery Responses To Special And Form Interrogatories, Requests For Document Production And To Produce Documents. Plaintiff's unopposed discovery motion is granted for good cause shown in the moving papers. Defendant Tracy Dearman is ordered to respond to the following discovery within two weeks of notice of entry of this order: (1) Special Interrogatories, Set One; (2) Special Interrogatories, Set Two; (3) Form Interrogatories Set Two; (4) Requests for Production Set Two; and (5) Form Interrogatories Set Three. Defendant TCGSF, Inc. is ordered to respond to the following discovery within two weeks of notice of entry of this order: (1) Special Interrogatories, Set One; and (2) Form Interrogatories Set Two. Requests for Admission to Dearman, sets two and three, are deemed admitted by Dearman. Requests for Admission to TCGSF, set two, are deemed admitted by TCGSF. Sanctions are mandatory here. (Code Civ. Proc., sec. 2033.280, subd. (c).) The court orders TCGSF and Dearman jointly and severally to pay $2784.50 to Plaintiff within 30 days of notice of entry of this order. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA).

rulings.Case Title: ANA MARIA CUEVAS VS. INTERNATIONAL TRAINING & EXCHANGE, INC. D/B/A ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Amended Motion To Dismiss Entire Paga Action Without Prejudice; Declaration Of Joseph C. Ramli; [Proposed] Order
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, April 8, 2026, Line 2, PLAINTIFF ANA CUEVAS, THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA, AND OTHER AGGRIEVED EMPLOYEES' Amended Motion To Dismiss Entire PAGA Action Without Prejudice. The unopposed motion to dismiss the entire PAGA action without prejudice is granted. The court finds that on the facts presented here, and for good cause shown in the moving papers, the settlement of Plaintiff's individual claim and the dismissal without prejudice of all claims is fair and reasonable in view of PAGA's purposes. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. rulings.Case Title: CUAUHTEMOC RAMIREZ CASTANEDA VS. ARTISTIC GEOMETRY, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Approval Of Settlement Under Private Attorneys General Act
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, April 8, 2026, Line 3, PLAINTIFF CUAUHTEMOC CASTANEDA's, Motion For Approval Of Settlement Under Private Attorneys General Act. The unopposed motion to approve the settlement is granted. The court finds that the settlement is "fair, reasonable, and adequate in view of PAGA's purposes to remediate present labor law violations, deter future ones, and to maximize enforcement of state labor laws." (Moniz v. Adecco USA, Inc. (2021) 72 Cal.App.5th 56.) The court approves the payment of civil penalties to the State and the Aggrieved Employees, approves Phoenix Settlement Administrators as the Settlement Administrator and payment of its fees in the amount of $3,490.00, approves payment of Plaintiff's counsel's attorney's fees in the amount of $11,666.67 and costs in the amount of $7,500.00, and approves the individual plaintiff's service award. Plaintiff is directed to resubmit the proposed stipulation to file a First Amended Complaint and to submit a proposed order making any necessary findings and directing the settlement administrator to proceed. The proposed order should be first be submitted to the other party (CRC 3.1312) and should contain a final compliance hearing date and a direction to the settlement administrator to submit a declaration attesting to disbursement of the settlement proceeds at least a week in advance of the final compliance hearing date. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA).

rulings.Case Title: INFLECT INC. VS. LUMEN TECHNOLOGIES GROUP ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant And Cross-Complainant Centurylink Communications, Llc Dba Lumen Technology Services Group'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, April 8, 2026, Line 4, DEFENDANT CENTURYLINK COMMUNICATIONS, LLC'S Motion For Protective Order. Centurylink Communications, LLC d/b/a Lumen's motion for protective order is granted. The court sets June 30, 2026 as the production deadline for all parties to complete production on existing document requests; this deadline is based on the parties' production commitments made at the informal discovery conference of April 2, 2026. Inflect Inc.'s deposition of Dan Albert may be noticed for a date after June 30, 2026. Good cause is shown: Lumen wishes to depose Albert with the benefit of documents that are not yet produced. Inflect does not articulate a particular reason why Albert's deposition should occur before production. The court does not award sanctions, finding all parties' positions had substantial justification. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. rulings.Case Title: WELLS FARGO BANK, NA VS. ADAN BALTAZAR
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Deeming The Truth Of The Matters Specified In Plaintiffs Request For Admission As Admitted
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, April 8, 2026, Line 5, PLAINTIFF WELLS FARGO BANK, NA's Motion For An Order Deeming The Truth Of The Matters Specified In Plaintiffs Request For Admission As Admitted. Wells Fargo N.A.'s motion for a deemed-admitted order is granted as to the RFAs attached as Exhibit 1 to the Lopez Declaration. These RFAs were initially served while defendant Adan Baltazar was represented by counsel but Wells Fargo's moving papers show that plaintiff also served the discovery directly on the defendant on February 10, 2026 and no responses have been received. Sanctions are mandatory in this instance. (Code Civ. Proc., sec. 2033.280, subd. (c).) The court orders Baltazar to pay $50 to plaintiff within 30 days. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.
